One of the things that's been cool about this change we've made is a lot of times any tool somewhat similar to this or how we used to do this in the past, if you get to one of these steps, something looks odd, you know, there's maybe a depth you're feeling is lacking on a certain subject, whatever it may be.
Usually, there's kind of two ways to approach it.
You can just rewrite it by hand or you can hypothesize what was it about the inputs that led to this output and then go back and rerun the workflows to try to get to it.
But at times, that can be challenging because you may lose some of the other good stuff happening to fix another problem or you didn't even fix the problem altogether.
What we've included here is...
So we different ways of being able to do this in line.
So you could always type it, but you could also use your AI chat tool and just say, hey, this is what I'm trying to do.
But we also have these fun shortcuts, things around like, hey, you know, simplify for this audience or verify technical accuracy or add technical examples.

And so we work and continue to build out all the different things, but it just arms our team with different ways of being able to move fast and apply a lot of the things that we have powered into this.
Finally, you know, we're just at time, but one of the things we found is quite critical to this is, you know, because of hallucinations or a whole variety of other challenges is deep fact checking.
So looking at what's happening in the background, jumping into our workflow engine, this has actually been quite powerful for us.
Essentially, our approach is we take the piece of content, we split it into varying passages.
So that's essentially what's going to happen here, extracting these different passages.
And then we go through and we verify each one with evidence.
So we're asking questions.
How do we verify that this is true?
What are the sources?
We can even point this to specific sources as needed.
We then score what is our confidence that this statement is correct.
So in this case, this is saying like anything above zero is positive.
In this case, this is saying correcting, giving context why.
Let's see if I can find one that's so this is like a zero.
Occasionally you'll find ones that are like a negative score and it's where this is actually incorrect.
And sometimes it's like really subtle nuance of phrasing that leads to that.
And so give context of why it will even rewrite it to make it more factually correct.
But one of the reasons it's been important for us, a lot of our content needs to pass legal review, medical review.
We do a lot of content for engineers, and so if you just say something slightly wrong, you can lose trust really fast.
And so it's things like this that have enabled us to basically start to move faster on accounts where the bar for technical content is quite a bit higher.
